summary: Iter20 QR-tracking feature fully validated. 11/11 pytest backend tests passed (BUG #1-#8) + 2/2 Playwright flows passed (BUG #9 Landing tracking with Android UA + BUG #10 Admin Stats QR tab via JWT bypass). All endpoints (POST /api/qr/scan public, GET /api/admin/qr/stats admin-only), aggregation pipelines, campaign lowercasing, untracked-campaign fallback, and 7-day window logic behave per spec. Frontend Landing properly persists signup_campaign to localStorage and fires fire-and-forget scan POST; AdminDashboard exposes qr-stats tab with correct data-testids and renders the full stats table.

minor.issue: Endpoint swallows DB insert errors and returns 200 {status:'ignored'} (intended graceful degradation, but loses observability — recommend logging metric or returning 503 in case of repeated failures).
No TTL index on scanned_at. Long-term the collection can grow unbounded. Already noted by E1 as backlog.

issues: Visual layout is clean — totals cards + table + Copier button work correctly. No contrast or alignment issues observed at 1920x1080.

critical_code_review_comments: qr_tracking.py:31-41 — Insert errors are silently swallowed (returns 'ignored'). Consider structured logging (campaign, error type) so admin sees ingest failures.

qr_tracking.py — No rate-limiting on the public POST /api/qr/scan; a bad actor could spam thousands of scan docs to skew stats. Consider IP-based throttling or a per-IP+per-campaign de-dup window (e.g. 1 scan per IP per campaign per 5 min) before promoting to prod.

qr_tracking.py:97 — conversion_pct uses raw scans denominator; this counts duplicate scans from a same visitor reloading the page. The 'scans' KPI is therefore page-views-with-?ref, not unique visitors. Acceptable for MVP, but should be documented in QRStatsTab UI tooltip.

Landing.js:24-41 — Tracking fetch is fire-and-forget with no de-dup on rapid double-mount (StrictMode dev or react 18 effect double-run produced 2 scans per page load in our test — 4 docs for 2 navigations). In production, consider sessionStorage flag to dedupe per session.

QRStatsTab.jsx:30 — baseUrl computed from window.location.origin will produce preview URLs (metro-taxi-demo.preview.emergentagent.com) when an admin uses preview env; for prod links it works fine but worth a note.
test_credentials: Admin OTP-bypass via JWT signing (JWT_SECRET from /app/backend/.env, admin id from db.admins collection). Same pattern as iter14/17/18/19. No production credentials needed for the test suite.
seed_data_creation: All test data prefixed test-iter20-* and test-playwright-* in qr_scans, users.signup_campaign, drivers.source_inscription. Cleanup fixture in test file + manual cleanup ran after Playwright (qr_scans: 4 deleted, users: 0, drivers: 0).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter20 closed. Reusable artifacts: test_iteration20_qr_tracking.py (11 backend tests with JWT admin bypass + cleanup fixture). For Playwright admin flows, the JWT-injection-into-localStorage trick under origin metro-taxi-demo.preview.emergentagent.com works reliably to bypass the email OTP step — token must include {user_id, role:'admin', exp} signed with JWT_SECRET. Pattern to reuse for future admin UI tests.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — feature works end-to-end. Only non-blocking observations listed under critical_code_review_comments (rate-limiting, dedup, silent error swallowing).
